mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side activity, improving corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST show positive momentum, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) during a recessionary regime reduces corporate revenues, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2% YoY, or baseline return distribution shifts above zero in affected periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates tight labor markets, driving corporate pricing power and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 30% of periods with simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline, or interaction effect size < 0.10 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic momentum, increasing corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 35% of periods with GDPC1 growth > 3%, or baseline return distribution shifts below zero in affected periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 100-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) within a 3-month window signals aggressive monetary tightening, reducing equity risk appetite and creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 1.0% in 3mo, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) and decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market deterioration, lowering corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 35% of periods with simultaneous UNRATE rise and PAYEMS decline, or interaction effect size < 0.08 in rolling regressions.
